skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Use the RunComfy CLI to extend a video canvas, change aspect ratio, and add matching background around the original frame.
skill.summary: Extend video canvases and change aspect ratios with RunComfy video outpainting guidance.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution and network access are true positives because the skill intentionally invokes the runcomfy CLI and uses RunComfy services. The weak cryptography detections are false positives in prose, and no prompt injection attempt or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution and Network Processing
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill intentionally runs the runcomfy CLI, posts requests to the RunComfy Model API, polls status, and downloads results. This is expected for the skill, but it requires command execution and third-party network processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown contains direct runcomfy command examples and states that the CLI posts to the Model API, polls, and downloads output. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the external command and network risk is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: User Media References and Prompts Leave Local Environment
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends video URLs and edit prompts to RunComfy for processing. Users should treat source videos and prompts as data shared with an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example input includes a video URL and prompt, and the documentation describes outbound API requests. No hidden exfiltration was found, but external processing is part of the workflow.

low_findings.title: Credential Token Stored in Hidden Configuration Path
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The security notes state that runcomfy login writes an API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is normal CLI behavior, but token handling should be disclosed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly documents token storage and permissions. The mode 0600 mitigation lowers severity, but the path contains a sensitive credential.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The lines flagged for weak cryptography contain prose, headings, or prompt guidance, not cryptographic functions or security decisions. No evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or similar algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspection of the flagged lines shows natural language and Markdown structure only. There is no cryptographic code or instruction to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Alerts Are Mostly Documentation Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Most hardcoded URLs are RunComfy documentation, model catalog, workflow, or related skill links. The example CDN URL is a placeholder, not a hidden destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs resolve to product documentation, model pages, workflow pages, or related marketplace skills. They do not show secret collection or unexpected outbound destinations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access Limited to RunComfy Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash(runcomfy *) and includes runcomfy CLI examples. This is a constrained command surface, but marketplace users should understand that the skill can execute RunComfy commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata and examples show a real external command surface. It is scoped to runcomfy and aligned with the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Allowlist Mentioned
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents RunComfy outbound endpoints and says there is no telemetry. This reduces ambiguity, but users still send processing requests outside the local machine.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The outbound service behavior is explicitly described in the skill. I did not find evidence of additional hidden endpoints.

content.value_statement: Changing aspect ratios can crop important action or leave empty borders. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through RunComfy video outpainting workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides RunComfy CLI usage for prompt-based video canvas expansion.
Targets Wan 2-7 Edit-Video for common aspect ratio changes.
Provides prompt patterns for preserving central action and framing.
Recommends ComfyUI outpainting workflows when seam quality is critical.
Documents relevant RunComfy exit codes and troubleshooting links.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and valid authentication.
Sends prompts and media references to RunComfy external services.
Cannot run GUI-only ComfyUI workflows through the CLI path.
Seam quality and temporal consistency can vary by clip and prompt.

use_cases.title: Convert Social Clips to Wide Video
use_cases.description: Expand vertical clips into horizontal layouts while keeping the subject centered.

use_cases.title: Prepare Brand Banners from Footage
use_cases.description: Add matching side environment around square or cropped footage for campaign assets.

use_cases.title: Assess Outpainting Workflow Fit
use_cases.description: Choose between the quick CLI route and dedicated ComfyUI workflows for quality needs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Aspect Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple vertical to horizontal conversion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video-outpainting skill to expand this vertical clip to 16:9. Preserve the main subject and keep motion natural.

prompt_templates.title: Social Clip Reframe
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with platform and framing requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare this short clip for YouTube. Extend the left and right background, keep the subject centered, and avoid changing clothing or face details.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Asset Expansion
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with art direction and quality expectations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this square product video into a wide banner. Continue the studio background, match lighting, and keep all product edges stable.

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Selection Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning request before running the job.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this clip and recommend the RunComfy route. Use Wan 2-7 for speed or a ComfyUI outpainting workflow if seams must be production quality.

output_examples.input: A vertical product clip needs a horizontal version for a landing page hero.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends side expansion, a prompt that preserves the product center, and a RunComfy output folder for review.

output_examples.input: A social editor wants to turn a TikTok clip into a YouTube short preview.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a 16:9 outpainting plan with matching environment, central action preservation, and quality checks for seams.

output_examples.input: A production team needs hero-grade video outpainting with strict seam quality.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a dedicated ComfyUI workflow instead of the quick CLI model path.

best_practices: Use user-provided video URLs only, and confirm that the content can be processed by RunComfy.
Lead prompts with the target canvas size, then describe the environment to extend.
Review seams and motion after each pass before running additional expansion passes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for confidential videos unless external processing is approved.
Do not expect CLI access to GUI-only ComfyUI workflows.
Do not run repeated outpainting passes without checking quality degradation.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides video outpainting with RunComfy, mainly for canvas expansion and aspect ratio changes.

faq.question: Does it process videos locally?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses RunComfy services through the runcomfy CLI.

faq.question: Which model does it use by default?
faq.answer: It points to Wan 2-7 Edit-Video for the closest CLI-accessible path.

faq.question: Can it handle hero-quality seams?
faq.answer: It recommends dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows when seam quality must be production grade.

faq.question: Does it support still image outpainting?
faq.answer: No. The skill is focused on video canvas expansion, not single-image outpainting.

faq.question: What should users check before running it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm RunComfy access, token handling, video permissions, and approval for external processing.
